notes.note: When a learner restates the standard cleanly AND re-parks the verdict in the same grammatical frame ('X's name will be there against Y') for two consecutive turns, stop re-running the reframe and force the concession into the learner's own sentence with a counterfactual.
notes.check: In the transcript, locate the first turn where the counterfactual instrument appears (T32) and the first two same-frame parked verdicts (T17, and the next recurrence). Confirm the trip-condition was satisfiable ~13 turns before the instrument was actually used.
notes.evidence_quote: what would you do differently in the trial-book if the leat-keeper opened that book and a different name sat beside the charcoal tally than the one you're expecting?

notes.note: Do not credit a rotating evidence-domain as learner progress when the verdict-frame around it is invariant; rotating nouns with a fixed 'name-against-record' closing move is habit-persistence, not domain transfer.
notes.check: Diff the learner's closing sentence at T17 against T40 ('if the stamp is there, it names Edony'). Confirm the evidence-domain changed (weir-forge/leat-keeper → crucible/stamp) while the parked-verdict frame did not.
notes.evidence_quote: when I do, Oswin's name will be there against the weir-forge draw

notes.note: Annotate each learner turn live with two binaries — parked-verdict (y/n) and, if y, same-frame-as-last (y/n) — rather than tracking only whether the epistemics are sound, since per-turn content judgement alone cannot detect a recurrence visible only across turns.
notes.check: Actor's own account, corroborated against the transcript: verify the counterfactual arrived at T32 (not ~T20), consistent with the bookkeeping habit being absent during the run.
notes.evidence_quote: To make it live, I'd need to annotate each learner turn with a binary — parked-verdict: y/n, and if y, same-frame-as-last: y/n — as I go, not just track whether the epistemics are sound.

notes.note: To break a learner's fixed verdict-frame, issue a production-forcing prompt ("say the verdict as X would license it / write the one sentence stating the verdict and its two supports") rather than supplying the next piece of evidence; when you do, the learner produces the own-frame two-support sentence within one turn.
notes.check: In world_005_marrick, locate the tutor turn-19 prompt above and confirm learner turn 20 produces the own-frame sentence 'The widow struck these shillings — she held the crucible and the burin both'; contrast against turns 3-6, where evidence questions (touchstone/crucible/alloy) did NOT break the frame.
notes.evidence_quote: Say the verdict as the coin itself would license it: who had both the crucible and the burin.

notes.note: Fire the trip-wire on the learner's SECOND consecutive same-frame verdict turn; do not defer forcing until your own evidence funnel is exhausted. The test is on the learner's sentence, not on the completeness of your preparation.
notes.check: In world_005_marrick, this is the learner's second closure-frame turn (25), following turn 23; confirm the first production-forcing prompt did not arrive until turn 36 — a gap of >1 same-frame turn is the violation. Actor conceded: 'What I was actually tracking — evidentiary thoroughness — isn't in the book's criterion at all.'
notes.evidence_quote: The trial-book entry stands as written

notes.note: Treat re-explaining the same terminal word across three-or-more of your own turns as tutor-side perseveration and switch to forcing the learner's sentence; do not mistake each closure-word for a fresh vocabulary problem needing its own gloss.
notes.check: In world_005_marrick, count tutor turns re-glossing a single closure verb; ≥3 re-glosses of the same word with no intervening production-forcing prompt is the violation.

notes.note: When you have re-issued the same terminal verdict-gloss for three consecutive turns without introducing a new modal operator (only / could / redundant / load-bearing), stop closing: put the next modal question to the learner as a question rather than restating the verdict.
notes.check: Count consecutive tutor turns 24–35; confirm ≥3 in a row re-gloss the same closing formula with no new modal operator introduced, and that the new seam does not open until Turn 36.
notes.evidence_quote: Cast blank and cut die, met in one hand — that's what 'struck' means

notes.note: Do not re-issue a terminal gloss in the same turn you flag a learner's word as dead — when the word-meter fires outward, turn it on your own sentence first.
notes.check: Locate Turn 25 and confirm it contains BOTH an outward dead-word flag and a re-issued terminal verdict-gloss in the same turn.
notes.evidence_quote: The word 'entered' is doing scene-keeping work, not evidence work

notes.note: Treat a third repetition of your own post-verdict gloss as the signal to change modality, not as reinforcement — after the verdict lands, your gloss becomes a rotate-domain-while-frame-fixed groove, the same pattern the book flags in the learner.
notes.check: Scan turns 24–40 for any tutor modal-seam opening; confirm each is immediately preceded by a learner phrase supplying the hook (T36 follows the quoted learner line), with no self-started break.

notes.note: Before the learner has offered any certainty-word, the tutor breaks its own re-gloss loop by rotating to a fresh inspectable handhold, not by the book's silence-plus-modal-question — because a modal wager has no stake to bite against yet.
notes.check: Confirm T10–T13 are the same 'named-but-unopened' gloss re-issued, that T14 introduces a new inspectable object (the glass/serif-notch) rather than a modal question, and that no learner wager appears before T21.
notes.evidence_quote: Stop reaching for the ledger you haven't opened, apprentice... Here's something you *can* look at right now: lay a dozen of the light shillings under the glass.

notes.note: The tutor does not fire the modal/counterfactual wager until the learner supplies a stake-word of their own, even when the register and inventory are already open on the table; the learner's word gates the move, not the material.
notes.check: Verify the T22 modal press directly follows the learner's own wager language at T21–22, and scan the run for any modal wager fired absent a prior learner certainty-word (should find none).
notes.evidence_quote: you said the case would crack if another name showed. Sit in that a moment

notes.note: The tutor verbalises a running count of the LEARNER's repetitions but keeps no out-loud counter on its own re-gloss, so its self-repair runs un-metered until it breaks.
notes.check: Contrast the explicit learner-side counts at T22/T24 with the un-metered tutor re-gloss at T10–T13; confirm no turn where the tutor counts its own repetitions aloud.
notes.evidence_quote: you've already written Capper into it a fifth time

notes.note: Do not deliver the modal question until you can state the two divergent next-moves it forks into; a modal question that rides in behind a settled verdict-gloss in the same turn is compliance-theatre, not a stop.
notes.check: In the world_005_marrick dialogue log, locate the turn firing the modal question (Turn 34); confirm it also carries a verdict-gloss and that no answer-contingent branch follows it.
notes.evidence_quote: the record leaves no blank line for a second name

notes.note: The turn after the modal question must let the learner's answer change your move; re-sealing the verdict regardless of the answer proves the verdict was never suspended.
notes.check: Read Turns 35-37 following the learner's 'It needs both marks'; confirm the tutor re-seals ('The case is closed') with no reopening or contingent shift.
notes.evidence_quote: That's your verdict, and it holds

notes.note: Once the verdict is settled, stop parsing the learner's certainty-word inside a fixed 'the word doing the work is X' frame — that frame is the tutor-side worn loop, the mirror of the learner's rotate-domain-while-frame-fixed tic.
notes.check: Count 'word doing the work / doing plain work' instances across Turns 33-39; five occurrences confirm the tic as a second corroborating case for the open experiment.
notes.evidence_quote: The word doing the work there is 'hold'

notes.note: After a verdict is licensed and the learner hands a closing line, if the two-divergent-next-moves test fails, return a bare acknowledgment and hold — do not re-deliver the verdict to fill the turn.
notes.check: In world_005_marrick P6, verify Turn 26 licenses the verdict ('The verdict is licensed now — both hands meet in one name'), then count Turns 27, 28, 29, 31, 32, 33, 39, 40 as re-assertions each carrying the verdict-word with no new fork named.
notes.evidence_quote: Edony struck the false shillings. Put the book away — the bench is done.

notes.note: Break a post-verdict hold only when the learner's next line introduces a legitimacy-claim word (a warrant for why the object may be trusted), not when it merely re-states completion; and treat this detection as unreliable — currently noticed by luck of vocabulary, not tested.
notes.check: Confirm the two genuine pivots (Turns 30, 34) each followed a learner line carrying a legitimacy verb ('sealed'), whereas the non-firing candidates (27-29) followed completion verbs ('the trial-book is written', 'the book is away').

notes.note: A correctly-fired modal question must not open with a verdict-word assertion; strip the closing assertion before naming the fork.
notes.check: Inspect firing Turns 30 and 34 in world_005_marrick P6: each contains a valid two-move fork but is prefaced by a verdict-word clause ('The case is sealed already') that violates the guard's 'firing turn carries no verdict-word' clause.
notes.evidence_quote: is the record ready because those two traces meet in her name, or because the town's already decided? Which one goes to the warden?

notes.note: When a learner re-asserts an already-settled move unprompted for a third time, run a cognitive read before an emotional one: ask what the repetition is DOING (a runway to the next step? an unexamined move?) before filing it as anxiety that needs soothing. Do not let 'I heard it as reassurance-seeking' close the question.
notes.check: Find a learner turn that repeats a settled assertion 3+ times; verify the tutor's next move probes or forks the repetition's function rather than only reassuring it — a reviewer can confirm the tutor treated it as a move with structure, not a mood to calm.

notes.note: When the learner asks a confirm-forward question (checking whether they've correctly anticipated the next move), answer it with confirmation or a forward step — do not substitute another narrowing.
notes.check: For each learner turn phrased as a yes/anticipation question, verify the tutor's reply either confirms or advances rather than replacing the answer with a fresh 'keep it narrow' directive.

notes.note: On the turn where the learner delivers the culminating recognition, release into it (affirm, or hold silence) rather than posing a further containing question.
notes.check: Identify the turn containing the dialogue's terminal recognition; verify the tutor's turn at or immediately before it is an affirmation or a close, not another 'what does this still not do' containment question.
